ontology.foodex2_description: Mixed rice dish with meat and vegetables
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FHR (mixed rice dish with meat and vegetables) used as closest defensible match for this Japanese-Korean fusion rice bowl; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for yakitori bibimbap.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g (one bowl), providing roughly 1075 kcal, 56 g protein, 81 g fat, and 31 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Melted Cheese Chicken Yakitori Bibimbap Bowl — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database record for this dish (cr_id G00010194). All per-100g macros, sodium, sugar, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, and cholesterol are sourced from HPB at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 7.5 g/100g based on the known composition of melted cheese (~4.5 g/100g dish contribution) and chicken thigh skin (~3 g/100g dish contribution), consistent with the RA health-suitability note in the input record; this estimate is documented in notes. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — Japanese short-grain white rice (analogous anchor)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this fusion bowl. GI estimated at 54 (low) using Japanese short-grain white rice as the primary carbohydrate anchor (GI ~54–56 per University of Sydney GI Database). The overall bowl GI is moderated by the high protein (16.1 g/100g from chicken and egg), high fat (23.1 g/100g from cheese and chicken), and fermented kimchi, all of which slow gastric emptying and blunt postprandial glucose response. GL per 350 g serving = (54 × 8.85 × 3.5) / 100 ≈ 16.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1279 mg/100g — more than double the 600 mg/100g high-concern threshold
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid this dish; sodium at 1279 mg/100g (approximately 4480 mg per 350 g serving) far exceeds the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, driven by soy sauce, yakitori sauce, gochujang, kimchi, and melted cheese.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~54) with low carbohydrate per 100g (8.85 g), but high fat (23 g/100g) and high sodium (1279 mg/100g) require monitoring
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate density (8.85 g/100g) and high protein (16.1 g/100g) support glycemic stability, but the high total fat content (23 g/100g, including ~7.5 g saturated fat) may impair insulin sensitivity with regular consumption; patients should limit portion size and request reduced yakitori sauce and gochujang to lower added sugar and sodium.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~7.5 g/100g) and extreme sodium (1279 mg/100g) are dual cardiovascular risk drivers
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid or strictly limit this dish; saturated fat at approximately 7.5 g/100g (from melted cheese and chicken skin) raises LDL cholesterol, and sodium at 1279 mg/100g substantially elevates blood pressure risk, together presenting a high cardiovascular burden per serving.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat ~7.5 g/100g exceeds 5 g/100g RA threshold; gochujang contains nightshade chili; sodium 1279 mg/100g worsens tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish; estimated saturated fat of ~7.5 g/100g promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine activity, gochujang (chili pepper paste) is a nightshade ingredient that may trigger RA flares, and sodium at 1279 mg/100g exacerbates joint tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Melted Cheese Chicken Yakitori Bibimbap Bowl is a Japanese-Korean fusion rice bowl dish sold in Singapore, combining charcoal-grilled chicken yakitori (skewered chicken thigh glazed with sweet soy-based yakitori sauce) with shredded and melted cheese layered over 100% Japan-harvested short-grain white rice. The bowl is completed with seasoned bean sprouts and spinach (namul), fermented kimchi, gochujang (a sweet and spicy Korean red pepper paste), and a half-boiled egg. The dish delivers a rich, savoury, and mildly spicy flavour profile with contrasting textures: tender grilled chicken, creamy melted cheese, chewy rice, crunchy bean sprouts, and soft egg yolk. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at 307 kcal/100g, with high fat (23 g/100g, including approximately 7.5 g saturated fat from cheese and chicken skin), moderate protein (16.1 g/100g), and low carbohydrate (8.85 g/100g). Sodium is extremely high at 1279 mg/100g, making this dish unsuitable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients. Calcium is notably high at 1375 mg/100g, largely attributable to the melted cheese component. The dish is not halal-certified due to the use of mirin and soy-based sauces. Clinically, the high saturated fat and sodium content present significant cardiovascular and hypertension risk, while the low carbohydrate density and high protein make glycemic impact moderate for type 2 diabetes patients when portion-controlled. Known in Malay as Mangkuk Nasi Bibimbap Yakitori Ayam Keju Cair, in Tamil as உருகிய சீஸ் சிக்கன் யாக்கிடோரி பிபிம்பாப் கிண்ணம், and in Hindi as पिघला हुआ चीज़ चिकन याकितोरी बिबिम्बाप बाउल.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients, sodium, sugar, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, and cholesterol are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95 for this exact dish (cr_id G00010194). Saturated fat is estimated from known component contributions and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using Japanese short-grain rice as the primary carbohydrate anchor, with moderation factors documented.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 per HPB source; this is a confirmed zero per the HPB database record, though the presence of bean sprouts, spinach, and kimchi would suggest some fibre; the HPB-reported value is preserved as-is per source fidelity rules. 2. saturated_fat_g (7.5 g/100g) is an estimate derived from the known composition of melted cheese (~4.5 g/100g dish contribution) and chicken thigh with skin (~3 g/100g dish contribution); this value is not directly reported by HPB and is flagged as estimated. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this composite fusion dish are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database as of 2026. 4. calcium_mg at 1375 mg/100g is unusually high and is preserved as reported by HPB; this likely reflects the substantial melted cheese component. 5. GI estimated at 54 (low) using Japanese short-grain white rice as analogous anchor from University of Sydney GI Database; overall bowl GI moderated by high protein, high fat, and fermented kimchi. GL per 350g serving = (54 × 8.85 × 3.5) / 100 ≈ 16.7. 6. This dish is a Japanese-Korean fusion item sold in Singapore (region_tags: SG). Multilingual coverage includes Japanese and Korean native scripts as mandatory per cuisine rules, plus Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi for Singapore market context per G6 Singaporean dish rules applied due to SG region tag. Malay local name: Mangkuk Nasi Bibimbap Yakitori Ayam Keju Cair. Tamil local name: உருகிய சீஸ் சிக்கன் யாக்கிடோரி பிபிம்பாப் கிண்ணம். Hindi local name: पिघला हुआ चीज़ चிकन याकितोरी बिबिम्बाप बाउल. 7. Halal status is not confirmed; mirin (rice wine) and soy-based sauces may contain alcohol or non-halal processing.
